steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question. The underlined claim in Text 1 asserts that the UNESCO honor has automatically and obviously brought awareness and boosted tourism to Buenaventura. The question asks what the author of Text 2 would say in response to that claim — meaning which choice directly challenges or qualifies it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A city that UNESCO officially names as a City of Gastronomy should still make deliberate efforts to attract visitors [in order to boost] tourism.: Text 2 argues that cities must still create intentional marketing strategies to benefit from the designation, meaning the honor alone is not sufficient. This directly contradicts Text 1's claim that the benefits are automatic and obvious. ✓
  • Choice B — Many people worked together to help Buenaventura be recognized by UNESCO, but chefs deserve the most credit for Buenaventura's selection as a City of Gastronomy.: Text 2 says nothing about credit for the selection. This is off-topic relative to the underlined claim. ✗
  • Choice C — There are other benefits to being named a UNESCO City of Gastronomy besides increased tourism.: Text 2 does not discuss additional benefits beyond tourism; it focuses on whether tourism benefits happen automatically. ✗
  • Choice D — The naming of Buenaventura as a City of Gastronomy was long overdue, given the city's delicious food.: Text 2 does not evaluate whether the honor was timely or deserved. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
